description_long: This is a medium-sized commercial airplane with a predominantly white fuselage accented by blue details on the nose and a blue stripe running along the windows. The aircraft features a pair of swept-back wings with red-tipped ends and underwing engines, as well as a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer. The cockpit is located at the front, characterized by a blue nose and multiple cockpit windows. The landing gear is visible beneath the wings and fuselage, suggesting a tricycle configuration. The airplane has several passenger windows along the sides, indicating its use for transporting people. The overall design is streamlined for efficient flight, with hard, smooth surfaces typical of modern airliners.
description: A white and blue commercial airplane with swept-back wings, underwing engines, and a streamlined fuselage featuring multiple passenger windows.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, blue nose, passenger windows, swept-back wings, and underwing engines.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail section, single vertical stabilizer, and the full wingspan with red-tipped ends.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the blue nose, passenger windows, and wing/engine configuration.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the blue cockpit nose, cockpit windows, and the forward sweep of the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept-back wings and red-tipped ends; no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all fixed and visible
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows at the front of the fuselage
